pattern: Because a closer is now two characters long, a single stray ')' can leave you needing either one more ')' to complete a pair or one more '(' to give an orphan pair a partner -- track both debts in one left-to-right pass.
hints: Scan left to right and keep a running count of '(' that haven't been closed yet -- call it `open`.

Whenever you hit a ')', look at whether its neighbour is also ')'. If not, you're missing one ')' to complete the pair, so add an insertion. Either way, that pair now needs an unmatched '(' to belong to -- if `open` is zero, that's another insertion, otherwise consume one `open`.
At the very end, every '(' still sitting unmatched needs a full two-character closer built from scratch, so add 2 for each one left in `open`.

edge_inputs.description: one opener with only a single matching closer
single character, an opener with nothing else
single character, a closer with nothing else
three consecutive closers after one opener -- only two belong to it
run of openers with no closers at all
lone closers interleaved with openers, alternating

stress.description: random strings of parentheses of varying density
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 100000))
    s = ''.join(rng.choice('((()))') for _ in range(n))
    return [s]
